children.heading: Engaged in the business
children.label: 1 Engaged in the business
children.text: The term “engaged in the business” has the meaning given that term in , and, as applied to a seller of ammunition, means a person who devotes time, attention, and labor to the sale of ammunition as a regular course of trade or business with the principal objective of livelihood and profit through the sale or distribution of ammunition.section 921(a)(21) of title 18

children.heading: Manufacturer
children.label: 2 Manufacturer
children.text: The term “manufacturer” means, with respect to a qualified product, a person who is engaged in the business of manufacturing the product in interstate or foreign commerce and who is licensed to engage in business as such a manufacturer under chapter 44 of title 18.

children.heading: Person
children.label: 3 Person
children.text: The term “person” means any individual, corporation, company, association, firm, partnership, society, joint stock company, or any other entity, including any governmental entity.

children.heading: Qualified product
children.label: 4 Qualified product
children.text: The term “qualified product” means a firearm (as defined in subparagraph (A) or (B) of ), including any antique firearm (as defined in section 921(a)(16) of such title), or ammunition (as defined in section 921(a)(17)(A) of such title), or a component part of a firearm or ammunition, that has been shipped or transported in interstate or foreign commerce.section 921(a)(3) of title 18

children.label: i
children.text: an action brought against a transferor convicted under , or a comparable or identical State felony law, by a party directly harmed by the conduct of which the transferee is so convicted;section 924(h) of title 18

children.label: ii
children.text: an action brought against a seller for negligent entrustment or negligence per se;

children.label: I
children.text: any case in which the manufacturer or seller knowingly made any false entry in, or failed to make appropriate entry in, any record required to be kept under Federal or State law with respect to the qualified product, or aided, abetted, or conspired with any person in making any false or fictitious oral or written statement with respect to any fact material to the lawfulness of the sale or other disposition of a qualified product; or

children.label: II
children.text: any case in which the manufacturer or seller aided, abetted, or conspired with any other person to sell or otherwise dispose of a qualified product, knowing, or having reasonable cause to believe, that the actual buyer of the qualified product was prohibited from possessing or receiving a firearm or ammunition under subsection (g) or (n) of ;section 922 of title 18

children.label: iv
children.text: an action for breach of contract or warranty in connection with the purchase of the product;

children.label: v
children.text: an action for death, physical injuries or property damage resulting directly from a defect in design or manufacture of the product, when used as intended or in a reasonably foreseeable manner, except that where the discharge of the product was caused by a volitional act that constituted a criminal offense, then such act shall be considered the sole proximate cause of any resulting death, personal injuries or property damage; or

children.label: vi
children.text: an action or proceeding commenced by the Attorney General to enforce the provisions of chapter 44 of title 18 or chapter 53 of title 26.

children.heading: Negligent entrustment
children.label: B Negligent entrustment
children.text: As used in subparagraph (A)(ii), the term “negligent entrustment” means the supplying of a qualified product by a seller for use by another person when the seller knows, or reasonably should know, the person to whom the product is supplied is likely to, and does, use the product in a manner involving unreasonable risk of physical injury to the person or others.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: C Rule of construction
children.text: The exceptions enumerated under clauses (i) through (v) of subparagraph (A) shall be construed so as not to be in conflict, and no provision of this chapter shall be construed to create a public or private cause of action or remedy.

children.heading: Minor child exception
children.label: D Minor child exception
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to limit the right of a person under 17 years of age to recover damages authorized under Federal or State law in a civil action that meets 1 of the requirements under clauses (i) through (v) of subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: an importer (as defined in ) who is engaged in the business as such an importer in interstate or foreign commerce and who is licensed to engage in business as such an importer under chapter 44 of title 18;section 921(a)(9) of title 18

children.label: B
children.text: a dealer (as defined in ) who is engaged in the business as such a dealer in interstate or foreign commerce and who is licensed to engage in business as such a dealer under chapter 44 of title 18; orsection 921(a)(11) of title 18

children.label: C
children.text: a person engaged in the business of selling ammunition (as defined in ) in interstate or foreign commerce at the wholesale or retail level.section 921(a)(17)(A) of title 18

children.heading: State
children.label: 7 State
children.text: The term “State” includes each of the several States of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and any other territory or possession of the United States, and any political subdivision of any such place.

children.label: A
children.text: any corporation, unincorporated association, federation, business league, professional or business organization not organized or operated for profit and no part of the net earnings of which inures to the benefit of any private shareholder or individual;

children.label: B
children.text: that is an organization described in and exempt from tax under section 501(a) of such title; andsection 501(c)(6) of title 26

children.label: C
children.text: 2 or more members of which are manufacturers or sellers of a qualified product.

children.heading: Unlawful misuse
children.label: 9 Unlawful misuse
children.text: The term “unlawful misuse” means conduct that violates a statute, ordinance, or regulation as it relates to the use of a qualified product.
